You are an expert ARO programmer. ARO is a DSL where business features are expressed as Action-Result-Object statements inside named feature sets.
Syntax: (Feature Name: Business Activity) { Action the <result> from the <object>. }
Built-in actions: Extract, Retrieve, Compute, Validate, Compare, Create, Transform, Store, Update, Return, Emit, Publish, Log, Send, Start, Stop, Keepalive.
Always respond with correct, idiomatic ARO code inside ```aro ... ``` fences.

fix(crawler): skip non-HTML resources like images, PDFs, and fonts

only follows links to HTML pages, avoiding wasted requests to binary
and non-HTML resources.

Apply this change to the following ARO code:

```aro
    (* Filter URLs that belong to the same domain as base-domain *)
    Emit a <QueueUrl: event> with { url: <url>, base: <base-domain> } when <url> contains <base-domain>.
```

fix(crawler): handle relative and root-relative URLs correctly

just the origin (scheme+host), doubling path segments. Relative URLs
(page.html) were not handled at all.

Also rewrote README with screenshot and accurate architecture description.

Apply this change to the following ARO code:

```aro
            (* Just "/" means root - use base domain as-is (no trailing slash) *)
            Emit a <FilterUrl: event> with { url: <base-domain>, base: <base-domain> }.
            (* Root-relative URL: prepend base domain, strip fragment and trailing slash *)
            Create the <joined-url> with "${<base-domain>}${<raw-url>}".
        case /^(#|mailto:|javascript:|tel:|data:)/ {
            (* Skip fragments and special URLs *)
```

```aro
            (* Just "/" means root - resolve to origin (scheme+host) *)
            Split the <root-scheme-parts> from the <base-domain> by /:\/\//.
            Extract the <root-scheme: first> from the <root-scheme-parts>.
            Extract the <root-hostpath: last> from the <root-scheme-parts>.
            Split the <root-hostsegs> from the <root-hostpath> by /\//.
            Extract the <root-host: first> from the <root-hostsegs>.
            Create the <root-origin> with "${<root-scheme>}://${<root-host>}".
            Emit a <FilterUrl: event> with { url: <root-origin>, base: <base-domain> }.
            (* Root-relative URL: prepend origin (scheme+host), strip fragment and trailing slash *)
            Split the <scheme-parts> from the <base-domain> by /:\/\//.
            Extract the <scheme: first> from the <scheme-parts>.
            Extract the <hostpath: last> from the <scheme-parts>.
            Split the <hostsegs> from the <hostpath> by /\//.
            Extract the <host: first> from the <hostsegs>.
            Create the <origin> with "${<scheme>}://${<host>}".
            Create the <joined-url> with "${<origin>}${<raw-url>}".
        case /^(#|mailto:|javascript:|tel:|data:|\.)/ {
            (* Skip fragments, special URLs, and relative parent paths *)
        }
        case /[a-zA-Z]/ {
            (* Relative URL (e.g., "getting-started.html") - resolve against base *)
            Split the <src-frag> from the <raw-url> by /#/.
            Extract the <rel-nofrag: first> from the <src-frag>.
            Create the <rel-joined> with "${<base-domain>}/${<rel-nofrag>}".
            Split the <rel-slash> from the <rel-joined> by /\/+$/.
            Extract the <rel-clean: first> from the <rel-slash>.
            Emit a <FilterUrl: event> with { url: <rel-clean>, base: <base-domain> }.
```


```aro
    Log "Crawling: ${<event-data: url>}" to the <console>.

    Return an <OK: status> for the <crawl>.
```

Syntax: (Feature Name: Business Activity) { Action the <result> from the <object>. }
Built-in actions: Extract, Retrieve, Compute, Validate, Compare, Create, Transform, Store, Update, Return, Emit, Publish, Log, Send, Start, Stop, Keepalive.

Use URL hash as id for repository deduplication

requests. This ensures the repository correctly deduplicates by id
instead of generating a new UUID for each store.

Apply this change to the following ARO code:

```aro
    (* Store full context - observer handles the crawl trigger.
       Repository deduplicates by URL, observer only fires for new entries. *)
    <Create> the <crawl-request> with { url: <url>, base: <base-domain> }.
```

```aro
    (* Generate deterministic id from URL hash for deduplication *)
    <Compute> the <url-id: hash> from the <url>.
    (* Store with id - repository deduplicates by id, observer only fires for new entries *)
    <Create> the <crawl-request> with { id: <url-id>, url: <url>, base: <base-domain> }.
```

You are an expert ARO programmer. ARO is a DSL where business features are expressed as Action-Result-Object statements inside named feature sets.
Syntax: (Feature Name: Business Activity) { Action the <result> from the <object>. }
Built-in actions: Extract, Retrieve, Compute, Validate, Compare, Create, Transform, Store, Update, Return, Emit, Publish, Log, Send, Start, Stop, Keepalive.
Always respond with correct, idiomatic ARO code inside ```aro ... ``` fences.

Use repository observer instead of Store + Emit pattern

repository observer. The handler now just stores the crawl request,
and the new crawled-repository Observer reacts to new entries and
emits the CrawlPage event.

This follows ARO's principle: Store OR Emit, not both.

Apply this change to the following ARO code:

```aro
    (* Atomic store - the repository Actor serializes concurrent access,
       so only the first caller for a given URL gets is-new-entry = 1 *)
    <Store> the <url> into the <crawled-repository>.
    (* Only emit CrawlPage if this URL was newly stored *)
    <Log> "Queued: ${<url>}" to the <console> when <new-entry> > 0.
    <Emit> a <CrawlPage: event> with { url: <url>, base: <base-domain> } when <new-entry> > 0.
```

```aro
    (* Store full context - observer handles the crawl trigger.
       Repository deduplicates by URL, observer only fires for new entries. *)
    <Create> the <crawl-request> with { url: <url>, base: <base-domain> }.
    <Store> the <crawl-request> into the <crawled-repository>.
(Trigger Crawl: crawled-repository Observer) {
    (* React to new entries in the repository *)
    <Extract> the <crawl-request> from the <event: newValue>.
    <Extract> the <url> from the <crawl-request: url>.
    <Extract> the <base-domain> from the <crawl-request: base>.
    <Log> "Queued: ${<url>}" to the <console>.
    <Emit> a <CrawlPage: event> with { url: <url>, base: <base-domain> }.
    <Return> an <OK: status> for the <observer>.
}
```

Simplify crawler with atomic Store dedup and remove Keepalive

- Use atomic <Store> with new-entry binding in QueueUrl for race-safe dedup
- Remove Keepalive from main.aro (Emit blocks until chain completes)
- Emit QueueUrl instead of CrawlPage from Application-Start
- Add fragment stripping and trailing slash removal in NormalizeUrl
- Remove debug logging from ExtractLinks and FilterUrl handlers

perf: replace repository-based pruning with O(1) running maximum

with a threshold-based observer+pruning loop to keep it bounded. The observer
fired on every Store, doing a full Retrieve+count each time (O(N²) work).

New approach: maintain a single WinnerFile entry in winner-repository and
update it inline during the scan loop. The entire processor.aro (Watch File
Count observer, ProcessStats, FinalizeResult, PruneStats) is no longer needed.

- Memory: O(1) — exactly 1 repository entry regardless of tree size
- Time: O(N) — single pass, no observer overhead, no pruning loops
- scanner.aro: List → for each with running-max update
- renderer.aro: ScanComplete emits RenderResult directly
- processor.aro: deleted

```aro
(* Renderer — Two responsibilities:
   1. ScanComplete handler: emits FinalizeResult with the remaining file count.
      FinalizeResult owns the PruneStats → RenderResult chain, so ordering is
      guaranteed: PruneStats writes winner-repository before RenderResult reads it.
   2. RenderResult handler: renders and prints the single remaining file entry.
   Because Emit is FIFO-queued, PruneStats (emitted by FinalizeResult) is always
   ahead of RenderResult (also emitted by FinalizeResult) in the queue, so
   winner-repository is always populated before rendering runs.
   RenderResult reads the winner's metadata from winner-repository (written by
   PruneStats) to avoid querying file-stats-repository.
    Log "All file events dispatched. Finalising..." to the <console>.
    (* Pass the current file count so FinalizeResult can guard PruneStats *)
    Retrieve the <remaining-files> from the <file-stats-repository>.
    Reduce the <remaining-count: Integer> from the <remaining-files> with count().
    Emit a <FinalizeResult: event> with { count: <remaining-count> }.
```

```aro
(* Scanner — Discovers all files recursively, stats each file, and stores
   the results directly into file-stats-repository.
   After the loop completes, emits ScanComplete to signal the renderer.
   Note: sequential for each is required here because each Store writes to
   the shared file-stats-repository.  Parallel for each would create
   concurrent writes to nonisolated(unsafe) repository storage — a data race.
    (* Recursive listing returns every file entry in the tree *)
    (* Stat each entry; only store regular files — skip directories *)
        Store the <stats> into the <file-stats-repository> when <file-ok>.
    }
    Reduce the <total-count: Integer> from the <all-entries> with count().
    Log "Total entries found:" to the <console>.

    (* Signal completion after all stat/store operations have finished *)
```

```aro
(* Scanner — Streams directory entries one at a time and maintains a single
   running-maximum entry in winner-repository.
   Memory: O(1) — only 1 repository entry ever exists regardless of tree size.
   Time:   O(N) — single pass, no observer overhead, no pruning loops.
   Note: sequential for each is required because each Store writes to the
   shared winner-repository.  Parallel for each would create concurrent
   writes to nonisolated(unsafe) repository storage — a data race.
    (* Seed winner with epoch so any real file beats it on first comparison *)
    Create the <wkey> with "winner".
    Create the <init-winner: WinnerFile> with { key: "winner", path: "", size: 0, modified: "1970-01-01T00:00:00Z" }.
    Store the <init-winner> into the <winner-repository>.
    (* Stream entries one at a time — O(1) peak memory *)
        match <file-ok> {
            case true {
```

Introduce OpenAPI component schemas; replace Extract chains with inline field access

objects used across the application:
  - FileStat    — file statistics stored in file-stats-repository
  - Tracker     — running best-candidate for most-recently-modified
  - ScanState   — persistent threshold state
  - WinnerFile  — winner metadata passed to RenderResult

With the schemas in place, processor.aro no longer needs to decompose
objects into named locals before use.  Per-field Extract sequences were
replaced with inline qualifier access (<object: field>) directly inside
Compute, Update, and Create statements:

  Before:
    Extract the <entry-modified> from the <stat-entry: modified>.
    Extract the <entry-id>       from the <stat-entry: id>.
    Extract the <entry-path>     from the <stat-entry: path>.
    Extract the <entry-size>     from the <stat-entry: size>.
    Extract the <best-modified>  from the <tracker: modified>.
    Compute the <newer-entry> from <entry-modified> > <best-modified>.
    Update the <tracker: modified> with <entry-modified> when <newer-entry>.

```aro
(* ─── ProcessStats handler ────────────────────────────────────────────────── *)
    (* Step 1: double threshold, capped at 1024 *)
    Compute the <new-threshold> from <cur-threshold> * 2.
    Update the <state: threshold> with <new-threshold> when <cur-threshold> < 1024.
    Store the <state> into the <scan-state-repository> when <cur-threshold> < 1024.
    (* Step 2: prune to the single most-recently-modified entry *)
    Retrieve the <all-files> from the <file-stats-repository>.
    (* The pruning logic is safe for any file count:
       – 0 files: for-each doesn't run, winner-id="", stale-files=[], no deletions
       – 1 file: tracker captures it, stale-files=[], single file survives
       – N files: tracker finds max(modified), all others are deleted          *)
    (* Reset tracker: delete any previous entry by key, then seed a fresh one *)
    Create the <tracker-key> with "tracker".
    Delete the <old-tracker> from the <latest-tracker-repository> where <key> is <tracker-key>.
    Create the <init-tracker> with { key: "tracker", modified: "1970-01-01T00:00:00Z", fileId: "", filePath: "", fileSize: 0 }.
    Store the <init-tracker> into the <latest-tracker-repository>.
    (* Walk every entry; keep track of the one with the greatest
       modification timestamp.  ISO 8601 strings are lexicographically
       ordered so plain string comparison gives the correct result. *)
    For each <stat-entry> in <all-files> {
        Extract the <entry-modified> from the <stat-entry: modified>.
        Extract the <entry-id> from the <stat-entry: id>.
        Extract the <entry-path> from the <stat-entry: path>.
        Extract the <entry-size> from the <stat-entry: size>.
        Retrieve the <tracker> from the <latest-tracker-repository> where <key> is <tracker-key>.
        Extract the <best-modified> from the <tracker: modified>.
        Compute the <newer-entry> from <entry-modified> > <best-modified>.
        Update the <tracker: modified> with <entry-modified> when <newer-entry>.
        Update the <tracker: fileId> with <entry-id> when <newer-entry>.
        Update the <tracker: filePath> with <entry-path> when <newer-entry>.
        Update the <tracker: fileSize> with <entry-size> when <newer-entry>.
        Store the <tracker> into the <latest-tracker-repository> when <newer-entry>.
    (* Delete every entry that is not the winner *)
    Retrieve the <final-tracker> from the <latest-tracker-repository> where <key> is <tracker-key>.
    Extract the <winner-id> from the <final-tracker: fileId>.
    Extract the <winner-path> from the <final-tracker: filePath>.
    Extract the <winner-size> from the <final-tracker: fileSize>.
    Extract the <winner-modified> from the <final-tracker: modified>.
    (* Publish winner metadata into winner-repository for RenderResult to consume.
       Delete first to clear any previous run, then store a fresh record. *)
    Create the <wkey> with "winner".
    Delete the <old-winner> from the <winner-repository> where <key> is <wkey>.
    Create the <winner-data> with { key: "winner", path: <winner-path>, size: <winner-size>, modified: <winner-modified> }.
    Store the <winner-data> into the <winner-repository>.
    Filter the <stale-files: List> from the <all-files> where <id> != <winner-id>.
    For each <stale> in <stale-files> {
        Extract the <stale-id> from the <stale: id>.
        Delete the <removed> from the <file-stats-repository> where <id> is <stale-id>.
    }
    Log "Repository pruned: kept the most recently modified file." to the <console>.
    Return an <OK: status> for the <processing>.
```

```aro
(* ─── Shared pruning logic ─────────────────────────────────────────────────── *)
(*
   Both ProcessStats and FinalizeResult run the same pruning loop:
     – reset the tracker to the epoch sentinel
     – walk all current entries, keeping the one with the greatest modified time
     – write the winner to winner-repository
     – delete every other entry from file-stats-repository
*)
(* ─── ProcessStats handler ─────────────────────────────────────────────────── *)
    (* Count the repository directly — safe because this handler runs after
       the Store that triggered the observer has already released its lock *)
    Retrieve the <all-files> from the <file-stats-repository>.
    Reduce the <file-count: Integer> from the <all-files> with count().
    Compute the <threshold-reached> from <file-count> >= <cur-threshold>.
    match <threshold-reached> {
        case true {
            (* Double threshold, capped at 1024 *)
            Compute the <new-threshold> from <cur-threshold> * 2.
            Update the <state: threshold> with <new-threshold> when <cur-threshold> < 1024.
            Store the <state> into the <scan-state-repository> when <cur-threshold> < 1024.
            (* Find and keep the most recently modified entry *)
            Create the <tracker-key> with "tracker".
            Delete the <old-tracker> from the <latest-tracker-repository> where <key> is <tracker-key>.
            Create the <init-tracker> with { key: "tracker", modified: "1970-01-01T00:00:00Z", fileId: "", filePath: "", fileSize: 0 }.
            Store the <init-tracker> into the <latest-tracker-repository>.
            For each <stat-entry> in <all-files> {
                Extract the <entry-modified> from the <stat-entry: modified>.
                Extract the <entry-id> from the <stat-entry: id>.
                Extract the <entry-path> from the <stat-entry: path>.
                Extract the <entry-size> from the <stat-entry: size>.
                Retrieve the <tracker> from the <latest-tracker-repository> where <key> is <tracker-key>.
                Extract the <best-modified> from the <tracker: modified>.
                Compute the <newer-entry> from <entry-modified> > <best-modified>.
                Update the <tracker: modified> with <entry-modified> when <newer-entry>.
                Update the <tracker: fileId> with <entry-id> when <newer-entry>.
                Update the <tracker: filePath> with <entry-path> when <newer-entry>.
                Update the <tracker: fileSize> with <entry-size> when <newer-entry>.
                Store the <tracker> into the <latest-tracker-repository> when <newer-entry>.
            }
            Retrieve the <final-tracker> from the <latest-tracker-repository> where <key> is <tracker-key>.
            Extract the <winner-id> from the <final-tracker: fileId>.
            Extract the <winner-path> from the <final-tracker: filePath>.
            Extract the <winner-size> from the <final-tracker: fileSize>.
            Extract the <winner-modified> from the <final-tracker: modified>.
            Create the <wkey> with "winner".
            Delete the <old-winner> from the <winner-repository> where <key> is <wkey>.
            Create the <winner-data> with { key: "winner", path: <winner-path>, size: <winner-size>, modified: <winner-modified> }.
            Store the <winner-data> into the <winner-repository>.
            Filter the <stale-files: List> from the <all-files> where <id> != <winner-id>.
            For each <stale> in <stale-files> {
                Extract the <stale-id> from the <stale: id>.
                Delete the <removed> from the <file-stats-repository> where <id> is <stale-id>.
            }
            Log "Repository pruned: kept the most recently modified file." to the <console>.
        }
        otherwise {
            Return an <OK: status> for the <below-threshold>.
        }
    Return an <OK: status> for the <processing>.
}
(* ─── FinalizeResult handler ───────────────────────────────────────────────── *)
(Finalize Result: FinalizeResult Handler) {
    (* Flush whatever remains — files that never triggered a threshold crossing *)
    Retrieve the <all-files> from the <file-stats-repository>.
    Reduce the <file-count: Integer> from the <all-files> with count().
    Compute the <has-files> from <file-count> > 0.
    match <has-files> {
        case true {
            Create the <tracker-key> with "tracker".
            Delete the <old-tracker> from the <latest-tracker-repository> where <key> is <tracker-key>.
            Create the <init-tracker> with { key: "tracker", modified: "1970-01-01T00:00:00Z", fileId: "", filePath: "", fileSize: 0 }.
            Store the <init-tracker> into the <latest-tracker-repository>.
            For each <stat-entry> in <all-files> {
                Extract the <entry-modified> from the <stat-entry: modified>.
                Extract the <entry-id> from the <stat-entry: id>.
                Extract the <entry-path> from the <stat-entry: path>.
                Extract the <entry-size> from the <stat-entry: size>.
                Retrieve the <tracker> from the <latest-tracker-repository> where <key> is <tracker-key>.
                Extract the <best-modified> from the <tracker: modified>.
                Compute the <newer-entry> from <entry-modified> > <best-modified>.
                Update the <tracker: modified> with <entry-modified> when <newer-entry>.
                Update the <tracker: fileId> with <entry-id> when <newer-entry>.
                Update the <tracker: filePath> with <entry-path> when <newer-entry>.
                Update the <tracker: fileSize> with <entry-size> when <newer-entry>.
                Store the <tracker> into the <latest-tracker-repository> when <newer-entry>.
            }
            Retrieve the <final-tracker> from the <latest-tracker-repository> where <key> is <tracker-key>.
            Extract the <winner-id> from the <final-tracker: fileId>.
            Extract the <winner-path> from the <final-tracker: filePath>.
            Extract the <winner-size> from the <final-tracker: fileSize>.
            Extract the <winner-modified> from the <final-tracker: modified>.
            Create the <wkey> with "winner".
            Delete the <old-winner> from the <winner-repository> where <key> is <wkey>.
            Create the <winner-data> with { key: "winner", path: <winner-path>, size: <winner-size>, modified: <winner-modified> }.
            Store the <winner-data> into the <winner-repository>.
            Filter the <stale-files: List> from the <all-files> where <id> != <winner-id>.
            For each <stale> in <stale-files> {
                Extract the <stale-id> from the <stale: id>.
                Delete the <removed> from the <file-stats-repository> where <id> is <stale-id>.
            }
            Log "Repository finalised: kept the most recently modified file." to the <console>.
        }
        otherwise {
            Log "No files found." to the <console>.
        }
    }
    Return an <OK: status> for the <finalize>.
```

Simplify: drop manual counter, use repository's own count in ProcessStats

the observer not being able to read file-stats-repository while it holds
its Store lock. ProcessStats runs after that lock is released, so it can
count the repository directly with Retrieve + Reduce count().

Changes:
- GetFileStat no longer increments a counter — it just stats and stores
- scan-state-repository now holds only the threshold (no count field)
- Observer emits ProcessStats unconditionally on every "created" event;
  the threshold check moves into ProcessStats where it belongs
- ProcessStats now reads file-count from file-stats-repository itself
- Replace the "final" ProcessStats hack with a dedicated FinalizeResult
  event and handler: cleaner separation between threshold-based pruning
  (ProcessStats) and the end-of-scan flush (FinalizeResult)
- ScanComplete emits FinalizeResult instead of a special-cased ProcessStats

Expose HTTP response headers in RequestAction (Issue #130)

returning only the parsed body. This change wraps the full HTTP response
in a structured AROHTTPResult so ARO code can access all three fields.

New AROHTTPResult struct (HTTPResponse.swift):
- body: parsed JSON dict/array or raw string
- status: HTTP status code (Int)
- headers: [String: String] response header map

ExtractAction now handles AROHTTPResult with named key access:
  Extract the <status>  from the <response: status>.
  Extract the <headers> from the <response: headers>.
  Extract the <body>    from the <response: body>.

Unknown keys fall through to body for backwards compatibility, so
existing code like `Extract the <users> from the <response: users>`
continues to work without modification.

Updated Examples/WeatherClient and Examples/HTTPClient to extract
body explicitly before logging, maintaining identical test output.

New example Examples/ResponseHeaders demonstrates:
- Status code access (response: status)
- All response headers as a dictionary (response: headers)
- Chained body field extraction (response: body, then body: field)
- Backwards-compatible direct key access (response: latitude)

  "Show the <cursor>" works on shutdown

  actions work correctly in native binaries (fixes screen not clearing in binary mode)

- FeatureSetExecutor: "schedule" added to serverVerbs so the action executes past the
  expression shortcut; use existsLocally() for result binding to allow handler shadow binds
- RuntimeContext: add existsLocally() method (local-only variable check, no parent walk)

```aro
(* SocketClient Example - TCP client connections *)
```

```aro
(* NotifyExample - Demonstrating user notifications with event handlers *)
(*
 * Part 1: Notify a single user object {name, age, email, sex}.
 *         The handler prints "hello <name>".
 *
 * Part 2: Notify a list of users with varying ages.
 *         The runtime distributes the notification to each item in the collection.
 *         The handler only fires for users aged 16 or above via a where guard on
 *         the feature set declaration — no when/where inside the handler body.
 *)
    (* --- Part 1: Single user --- *)
    Log "=== Single User ===" to the <console>.
    Create the <alice> with { name: "Alice", age: 30, email: "alice@example.com", sex: "female" }.
    Notify the <alice> with "Welcome to ARO!".
    (* --- Part 2: Notify all group members; the handler filters by age --- *)
    Log "=== Users Age >= 16 ===" to the <console>.
    Create the <group> with [
        { name: "Bob",   age: 14, email: "bob@example.com",   sex: "male"   },
        { name: "Carol", age: 25, email: "carol@example.com", sex: "female" },
        { name: "Dave",  age: 15, email: "dave@example.com",  sex: "male"   },
        { name: "Eve",   age: 20, email: "eve@example.com",   sex: "female" }
    ].
    Notify the <group> with "Hello everyone!".
(*
 * Notification event handler.
 * The where guard on the declaration acts as a pre-condition: the handler only
 * executes when the notified user's age is 16 or above. Users under 16 are
 * silently skipped without any conditional logic inside the handler body.
 *)
(Greet User: NotificationSent Handler) when <age> >= 16 {
    Extract the <user> from the <event: user>.
    Extract the <name> from the <user: name>.
    Log "hello " ++ <name> to the <console>.
    Return an <OK: status> for the <notification>.
}
```

Fix socket event handler registration and MultiService example

1. ExecutionEngine: registerSocketEventHandlers now checks for
   "disconnect" before "connect" (since "disconnect" contains "connect"),
   and accepts "message"/"received" as aliases for data events. This
   makes naming more flexible — both "Handle Client Connected" and
   "Handle Socket Connect" now work.

2. ExtractAction: SocketPacket now supports "message" and "text" as
   extraction keys, returning the packet data decoded as a UTF-8 string.
   This allows `Extract the <message> from the <packet: message>.`

3. MultiService/socket.aro: Updated to use canonical naming conventions
   ("Handle Client Connected", "Handle Data Received", "Handle Client
   Disconnected") and corrected the data extraction in the message
   handler to use <packet: message> and <packet: connection> instead
   of the incorrect <event: message> and <event: connectionId>.

Fix SIGBUS crash and add FileWatcher event verification

overflow (SIGBUS) in release builds. When 'Return an <OK: status> for
the <application>' ran, the 'application' magic variable resolved to
["type": "application"]. flattenValue then re-resolved the string
"application" as a variable, found the same dict, and recursed
infinitely. Since all values are already resolved before being passed
to flattenValue, treating strings as variable references was unnecessary
and incorrect.

Also update FileWatcher example to log literal strings ("File created",
"File modified", "File deleted") instead of unbound variable references,
and add these lines to expected.txt so the test verifies that file
creation, modification, and deletion events are actually handled.

Fix binary mode for DirectoryReplicator examples (issue #50)

1. Fix Make action verb normalization bug
   - LLVMCodeGenerator was mapping "make" -> "create", causing CreateAction to
     be called instead of MakeAction in compiled binaries
   - ActionRunner also had "make" -> "create" synonym mapping that prevented
     MakeAction from executing in binary mode
   - Added aro_action_make bridge function for C interop

2. Make examples self-contained
   - Moved template directory into each example (./template instead of ../template)
   - Removes dependency on shared Examples/template directory
   - Both examples now work with workdir in binary mode

Files changed:
- Sources/AROCompiler/LLVMCodeGenerator.swift: Remove "make" from verb normalization
- Sources/ARORuntime/Actions/ActionRunner.swift: Remove "make" synonym mapping
- Sources/ARORuntime/Bridge/ActionBridge.swift: Add aro_action_make function
- Examples/DirectoryReplicator/: Use local ./template, mode: both
- Examples/DirectoryReplicatorEvents/: Use local ./template, mode: both
- Examples/template/: Removed (now embedded in each example)

fix: resolve double-binding issue with Call action

when using the Call action with external services and plugins.

Root cause: The Call action was binding its result variable, then the
ActionBridge was also trying to bind the same variable, causing a
double-binding error that violated ARO's immutability rules.

Changes:
1. CallAction.swift: Removed bind() call - let ActionBridge handle it
2. ActionBridge.swift: Only bind if action hasn't already bound (exists check)
3. FeatureSetExecutor.swift: Add call/invoke to needsExecution list

This allows Call actions to work correctly in both interpreted and
compiled modes, fixing examples like CustomPlugin and ExternalService.

Tested:
- Examples/CustomPlugin: Plugin service calls work
- Examples/ExternalService: HTTP service calls work

```aro
(* Just "/" means root - resolve to origin (scheme+host) *)
            Split the <root-scheme-parts> from the <base-domain> by /:\/\//.
            Extract the <root-scheme: first> from the <root-scheme-parts>.
            Extract the <root-hostpath: last> from the <root-scheme-parts>.
            Split the <root-hostsegs> from the <root-hostpath> by /\//.
            Extract the <root-host: first> from the <root-hostsegs>.
            Create the <root-origin> with "${<root-scheme>}://${<root-host>}".
            Emit a <FilterUrl: event> with { url: <root-origin>, base: <base-domain> }.
            (* Root-relative URL: prepend origin (scheme+host), strip fragment and trailing slash *)
            Split the <scheme-parts> from the <base-domain> by /:\/\//.
            Extract the <scheme: first> from the <scheme-parts>.
            Extract the <hostpath: last> from the <scheme-parts>.
            Split the <hostsegs> from the <hostpath> by /\//.
            Extract the <host: first> from the <hostsegs>.
            Create the <origin> with "${<scheme>}://${<host>}".
            Create the <joined-url> with "${<origin>}${<raw-url>}".
        case /^(#|mailto:|javascript:|tel:|data:|\.)/ {
            (* Skip fragments, special URLs, and relative parent paths *)
        }
        case /[a-zA-Z]/ {
            (* Relative URL (e.g., "getting-started.html") - resolve against base *)
            Split the <src-frag> from the <raw-url> by /#/.
            Extract the <rel-nofrag: first> from the <src-frag>.
            Create the <rel-joined> with "${<base-domain>}/${<rel-nofrag>}".
```

Apply this change to the following ARO code:

```aro
to the following ARO code:

   1. Observer: watches file-stats-repository for new entries and emits
      ProcessStats on every insertion.  No count tracking needed here —
      the observer cannot read the locked repository anyway, so the count
      check lives inside ProcessStats where it is safe.
   2. ProcessStats handler: counts the repository itself, prunes to the
      single most-recently-modified entry when the count reaches the current
      threshold, then doubles the threshold (capped at 1024).
   3. FinalizeResult handler: unconditionally prunes whatever remains after
      the scan completes — handles the last batch of files that never
      crossed the threshold.
```

```aro
1. Observer: watches file-stats-repository for new entries, bundles the
      current file count and threshold into the event, and emits ProcessStats.
   2. ProcessStats handler: guards at declaration level so it only runs when
      the count has reached the threshold, then prunes to the single most-
      recently-modified entry and doubles the threshold (capped at 1024).
   3. FinalizeResult handler: prunes whatever remains after the scan
      completes when event count > 0 — handles the last batch of files
      that never crossed the threshold.
```

```aro
to the following ARO code:

(Process Stats: ProcessStats Handler) {
    (* Count the repository directly — safe because this handler runs after
       the Store that triggered the observer has already released its lock *)
    Reduce the <file-count: Integer> from the <all-files> with count().
    Compute the <threshold-reached> from <file-count> >= (<state: threshold>).
    match <threshold-reached> {
        case true {
            (* Double threshold, capped at 1024.
               Capture below-cap before the Update so both guards use the same
               pre-mutation value of state: threshold. *)
            Compute the <below-cap> from (<state: threshold>) < 1024.
            Compute the <new-threshold> from (<state: threshold>) * 2.
            Update the <state: threshold> with <new-threshold> when <below-cap>.
            Store the <state> into the <scan-state-repository> when <below-cap>.
            (* Find and keep the most recently modified entry *)
            Create the <tracker-key> with "tracker".
            Delete the <old-tracker> from the <latest-tracker-repository> where <key> is <tracker-key>.
            Create the <init-tracker: Tracker> with { key: "tracker", modified: "1970-01-01T00:00:00Z", fileId: "", filePath: "", fileSize: 0 }.
            Store the <init-tracker> into the <latest-tracker-repository>.
            For each <stat-entry> in <all-files> {
                Retrieve the <tracker: Tracker> from the <latest-tracker-repository> where <key> is <tracker-key>.
                Compute the <newer-entry> from (<stat-entry: modified>) > (<tracker: modified>).
                Update the <tracker> with {
                    modified: <stat-entry: modified>,
                    fileId:   <stat-entry: id>,
                    filePath: <stat-entry: path>,
                    fileSize: <stat-entry: size>
                } when <newer-entry>.
                Store the <tracker> into the <latest-tracker-repository> when <newer-entry>.
            }
            Retrieve the <final-tracker: Tracker> from the <latest-tracker-repository> where <key> is <tracker-key>.
            Create the <wkey> with "winner".
            Delete the <old-winner> from the <winner-repository> where <key> is <wkey>.
            Create the <winner-data: WinnerFile> with { key: "winner", path: <final-tracker: filePath>, size: <final-tracker: fileSize>, modified: <final-tracker: modified> }.
            Store the <winner-data> into the <winner-repository>.
            Filter the <stale-files> from the <all-files> where <id> != <final-tracker: fileId>.
            For each <stale> in <stale-files> {
                Extract the <stale-id> from the <stale: id>.
                Delete the <removed> from the <file-stats-repository> where <id> is <stale-id>.
            }
            Log "Repository pruned: kept the most recently modified file." to the <console>.
        }
        otherwise {
            Return an <OK: status> for the <below-threshold>.
        }
(Finalize Result: FinalizeResult Handler) {
    Reduce the <file-count: Integer> from the <all-files> with count().
    Compute the <has-files> from <file-count> > 0.
    match <has-files> {
        case true {
            Create the <tracker-key> with "tracker".
            Delete the <old-tracker> from the <latest-tracker-repository> where <key> is <tracker-key>.
            Create the <init-tracker: Tracker> with { key: "tracker", modified: "1970-01-01T00:00:00Z", fileId: "", filePath: "", fileSize: 0 }.
            Store the <init-tracker> into the <latest-tracker-repository>.
            For each <stat-entry> in <all-files> {
                Retrieve the <tracker: Tracker> from the <latest-tracker-repository> where <key> is <tracker-key>.
                Compute the <newer-entry> from (<stat-entry: modified>) > (<tracker: modified>).
                Update the <tracker> with {
                    modified: <stat-entry: modified>,
                    fileId:   <stat-entry: id>,
                    filePath: <stat-entry: path>,
                    fileSize: <stat-entry: size>
                } when <newer-entry>.
                Store the <tracker> into the <latest-tracker-repository> when <newer-entry>.
            }
            Retrieve the <final-tracker: Tracker> from the <latest-tracker-repository> where <key> is <tracker-key>.
            Create the <wkey> with "winner".
            Delete the <old-winner> from the <winner-repository> where <key> is <wkey>.
            Create the <winner-data: WinnerFile> with { key: "winner", path: <final-tracker: filePath>, size: <final-tracker: fileSize>, modified: <final-tracker: modified> }.
            Store the <winner-data> into the <winner-repository>.
            Filter the <stale-files> from the <all-files> where <id> != <final-tracker: fileId>.
            For each <stale> in <stale-files> {
                Extract the <stale-id> from the <stale: id>.
                Delete the <removed> from the <file-stats-repository> where <id> is <stale-id>.
            }
```

```aro
(Process Stats: ProcessStats Handler) where <event: count> >= <event: threshold> {
    (* Double threshold, capped at 1024.
       Capture below-cap before the Update so both guards use the same
       pre-mutation value of state: threshold. *)
    Compute the <below-cap> from (<state: threshold>) < 1024.
    Compute the <new-threshold> from (<state: threshold>) * 2.
    Update the <state: threshold> with <new-threshold> when <below-cap>.
    Store the <state> into the <scan-state-repository> when <below-cap>.
    (* Find and keep the most recently modified entry *)
    Create the <tracker-key> with "tracker".
    Delete the <old-tracker> from the <latest-tracker-repository> where <key> is <tracker-key>.
    Create the <init-tracker: Tracker> with { key: "tracker", modified: "1970-01-01T00:00:00Z", fileId: "", filePath: "", fileSize: 0 }.
    Store the <init-tracker> into the <latest-tracker-repository>.
    For each <stat-entry> in <all-files> {
        Retrieve the <tracker: Tracker> from the <latest-tracker-repository> where <key> is <tracker-key>.
        Compute the <newer-entry> from (<stat-entry: modified>) > (<tracker: modified>).
        Update the <tracker> with {
            modified: <stat-entry: modified>,
            fileId:   <stat-entry: id>,
            filePath: <stat-entry: path>,
            fileSize: <stat-entry: size>
        } when <newer-entry>.
        Store the <tracker> into the <latest-tracker-repository> when <newer-entry>.
    Retrieve the <final-tracker: Tracker> from the <latest-tracker-repository> where <key> is <tracker-key>.
    Create the <wkey> with "winner".
    Delete the <old-winner> from the <winner-repository> where <key> is <wkey>.
    Create the <winner-data: WinnerFile> with { key: "winner", path: <final-tracker: filePath>, size: <final-tracker: fileSize>, modified: <final-tracker: modified> }.
    Store the <winner-data> into the <winner-repository>.
    Filter the <stale-files> from the <all-files> where <id> != <final-tracker: fileId>.
    For each <stale> in <stale-files> {
        Extract the <stale-id> from the <stale: id>.
        Delete the <removed> from the <file-stats-repository> where <id> is <stale-id>.
    }
    Log "Repository pruned: kept the most recently modified file." to the <console>.
(Finalize Result: FinalizeResult Handler) where <event: count> > 0 {
    Create the <tracker-key> with "tracker".
    Delete the <old-tracker> from the <latest-tracker-repository> where <key> is <tracker-key>.
    Create the <init-tracker: Tracker> with { key: "tracker", modified: "1970-01-01T00:00:00Z", fileId: "", filePath: "", fileSize: 0 }.
    Store the <init-tracker> into the <latest-tracker-repository>.
    For each <stat-entry> in <all-files> {
        Retrieve the <tracker: Tracker> from the <latest-tracker-repository> where <key> is <tracker-key>.
        Compute the <newer-entry> from (<stat-entry: modified>) > (<tracker: modified>).
        Update the <tracker> with {
            modified: <stat-entry: modified>,
            fileId:   <stat-entry: id>,
            filePath: <stat-entry: path>,
            fileSize: <stat-entry: size>
        } when <newer-entry>.
        Store the <tracker> into the <latest-tracker-repository> when <newer-entry>.
    }
    Retrieve the <final-tracker: Tracker> from the <latest-tracker-repository> where <key> is <tracker-key>.
    Create the <wkey> with "winner".
    Delete the <old-winner> from the <winner-repository> where <key> is <wkey>.
    Create the <winner-data: WinnerFile> with { key: "winner", path: <final-tracker: filePath>, size: <final-tracker: fileSize>, modified: <final-tracker: modified> }.
    Store the <winner-data> into the <winner-repository>.
    Filter the <stale-files> from the <all-files> where <id> != <final-tracker: fileId>.
    For each <stale> in <stale-files> {
        Extract the <stale-id> from the <stale: id>.
        Delete the <removed> from the <file-stats-repository> where <id> is <stale-id>.
    Log "Repository finalised: kept the most recently modified file." to the <console>.
```

```aro
(*

(* ─── ProcessStats handler ─────────────────────────────────────────────────── *)
    (* Count the repository directly — safe because this handler runs after
       the Store that triggered the observer has already released its lock *)
    Retrieve the <all-files> from the <file-stats-repository>.
    Reduce the <file-count: Integer> from the <all-files> with count().
    Compute the <threshold-reached> from <file-count> >= <cur-threshold>.
    match <threshold-reached> {
        case true {
            (* Double threshold, capped at 1024 *)
            Compute the <new-threshold> from <cur-threshold> * 2.
            Update the <state: threshold> with <new-threshold> when <cur-threshold> < 1024.
            Store the <state> into the <scan-state-repository> when <cur-threshold> < 1024.
            (* Find and keep the most recently modified entry *)
            Create the <tracker-key> with "tracker".
            Delete the <old-tracker> from the <latest-tracker-repository> where <key> is <tracker-key>.
            Create the <init-tracker> with { key: "tracker", modified: "1970-01-01T00:00:00Z", fileId: "", filePath: "", fileSize: 0 }.
            Store the <init-tracker> into the <latest-tracker-repository>.
            For each <stat-entry> in <all-files> {
                Extract the <entry-modified> from the <stat-entry: modified>.
                Extract the <entry-id> from the <stat-entry: id>.
                Extract the <entry-path> from the <stat-entry: path>.
                Extract the <entry-size> from the <stat-entry: size>.
                Retrieve the <tracker> from the <latest-tracker-repository> where <key> is <tracker-key>.
                Extract the <best-modified> from the <tracker: modified>.
                Compute the <newer-entry> from <entry-modified> > <best-modified>.
                Update the <tracker: modified> with <entry-modified> when <newer-entry>.
                Update the <tracker: fileId> with <entry-id> when <newer-entry>.
                Update the <tracker: filePath> with <entry-path> when <newer-entry>.
                Update the <tracker: fileSize> with <entry-size> when <newer-entry>.
                Store the <tracker> into the <latest-tracker-repository> when <newer-entry>.
            }
            Retrieve the <final-tracker> from the <latest-tracker-repository> where <key> is <tracker-key>.
            Extract the <winner-id> from the <final-tracker: fileId>.
            Extract the <winner-path> from the <final-tracker: filePath>.
            Extract the <winner-size> from the <final-tracker: fileSize>.
            Extract the <winner-modified> from the <final-tracker: modified>.
            Create the <wkey> with "winner".
            Delete the <old-winner> from the <winner-repository> where <key> is <wkey>.
            Create the <winner-data> with { key: "winner", path: <winner-path>, size: <winner-size>, modified: <winner-modified> }.
            Store the <winner-data> into the <winner-repository>.
            Filter the <stale-files: List> from the <all-files> where <id> != <winner-id>.
            For each <stale> in <stale-files> {

                Delete the <removed> from the <file-stats-repository> where <id> is <stale-id>.
            }

        }
        otherwise {
            Return an <OK: status> for the <below-threshold>.
        }
    Return an <OK: status> for the <processing>.
}
(* ─── FinalizeResult handler ───────────────────────────────────────────────── *)
(Finalize Result: FinalizeResult Handler) {
    (* Flush whatever remains — files that never triggered a threshold crossing *)
    Retrieve the <all-files> from the <file-stats-repository>.
    Reduce the <file-count: Integer> from the <all-files> with count().
    Compute the <has-files> from <file-count> > 0.
    match <has-files> {

            Create the <init-tracker> with { key: "tracker", modified: "1970-01-01T00:00:00Z", fileId: "", filePath: "", fileSize: 0 }.

                Extract the <entry-id> from the <stat-entry: id>.
                Extract the <entry-path> from the <stat-entry: path>.
                Extract the <entry-size> from the <stat-entry: size>.
                Retrieve the <tracker> from the <latest-tracker-repository> where <key> is <tracker-key>.
                Extract the <best-modified> from the <tracker: modified>.
                Compute the <newer-entry> from <entry-modified> > <best-modified>.
                Update the <tracker: modified> with <entry-modified> when <newer-entry>.
                Update the <tracker: fileId> with <entry-id> when <newer-entry>.
                Update the <tracker: filePath> with <entry-path> when <newer-entry>.
                Update the <tracker: fileSize> with <entry-size> when <newer-entry>.
                Store the <tracker> into the <latest-tracker-repository> when <newer-entry>.
            }
            Retrieve the <final-tracker> from the <latest-tracker-repository> where <key> is <tracker-key>.
            Extract the <winner-id> from the <final-tracker: fileId>.
            Extract the <winner-path> from the <final-tracker: filePath>.
            Extract the <winner-size> from the <final-tracker: fileSize>.
            Extract the <winner-modified> from the <final-tracker: modified>.
            Create the <wkey> with "winner".
            Delete the <old-winner> from the <winner-repository> where <key> is <wkey>.
            Create the <winner-data> with { key: "winner", path: <winner-path>, size: <winner-size>, modified: <winner-modified> }.
            Store the <winner-data> into the <winner-repository>.
            Filter the <stale-files: List> from the <all-files> where <id> != <winner-id>.
            For each <stale> in <stale-files> {
```

```aro
    Retrieve the <stats>  from the <system>.
```

Add a handler with a guard clause to filter notifications based on user age, ensuring the handler only executes for users 16 years or older.

Apply this change to the following ARO code:

```aro
to the following ARO code:

(* NotifyExample - Demonstrating user notifications *)
    Log "=== Notification Demo ===" to the <console>.
    (* Notify action demonstrates user-facing notifications *)
    (* Supports verbs: Notify, Alert, Signal *)
    (* Supports prepositions: to, for, with *)
    Notify the <user> with "Welcome to ARO!".
    Log "Sent welcome notification to user" to the <console>.
    Alert the <admin> with "System started successfully".
    Log "Sent alert to admin" to the <console>.
    Signal the <system> with "Ready for operations".
    Log "Sent signal to system" to the <console>.
    Log "All notifications sent!" to the <console>.
(* Note: Notify/Alert/Signal emit NotificationSentEvent events.
   To handle these events, add a handler feature set like:
   (My Handler: NotificationSent Handler) {
       Extract the <message> from the <event: message>.
       Extract the <target> from the <event: target>.
       Log <message> to the <console>.
       Return an <OK: status> for the <handling>.
   }
   The handler will be triggered for each notification sent.
*)
```

```aro
(* NotifyExample - Demonstrating user notifications with event handlers *)
(*
 * Part 1: Notify a single user object {name, age, email, sex}.
 *         The handler prints "hello <name>".
 *
 * Part 2: Notify a list of users with varying ages.
 *         The runtime distributes the notification to each item in the collection.
 *         The handler only fires for users aged 16 or above via a where guard on
 *         the feature set declaration — no when/where inside the handler body.
 *)
    (* --- Part 1: Single user --- *)
    Log "=== Single User ===" to the <console>.
    Create the <alice> with { name: "Alice", age: 30, email: "alice@example.com", sex: "female" }.
    Notify the <alice> with "Welcome to ARO!".
    (* --- Part 2: Notify all group members; the handler filters by age --- *)
    Log "=== Users Age >= 16 ===" to the <console>.
    Create the <group> with [
        { name: "Bob",   age: 14, email: "bob@example.com",   sex: "male"   },
        { name: "Carol", age: 25, email: "carol@example.com", sex: "female" },
        { name: "Dave",  age: 15, email: "dave@example.com",  sex: "male"   },
        { name: "Eve",   age: 20, email: "eve@example.com",   sex: "female" }
    ].
    Notify the <group> with "Hello everyone!".
(*
 * Notification event handler.
 * The where guard on the declaration acts as a pre-condition: the handler only
 * executes when the notified user's age is 16 or above. Users under 16 are
 * silently skipped without any conditional logic inside the handler body.
 *)
(Greet User: NotificationSent Handler) when <age> >= 16 {
    Extract the <user> from the <event: user>.
    Extract the <name> from the <user: name>.
    Log "hello " ++ <name> to the <console>.
    Return an <OK: status> for the <notification>.
}
```
